name: image_descriptions missing - derived from image_observations primary_observation
raw: {"thought":"The image shows a damp patch on a plastered wall just above the skirting board, with paint peeling away from the affected area and a darker tide-line running horizontally about 30 cm above floor level. The horizontal tide-line is characteristic of rising damp, where moisture wicks upwards from below the floor slab through capillary action. There is no visible plumbing in the immediate area and the ceiling above is dry, which rules out a roof or pipe leak as the source. The peeling paint and salts visible on the surface confirm the wall has been wet for an extended period.","diagnosis":"Rising Damp On Internal Wall","message":"The horizontal tide-line and salt staining on the wall is characteristic of rising damp — moisture is being drawn up from below the floor slab into the masonry through capillary action. The paint and plaster are failing because the wall has been wet for a sustained period.\n\nA proper damp-proof course injection and re-plastering with salt-resistant render is the durable fix. Painting over the patch alone will not resolve the underlying moisture path.","action_required":"","contractor_checklist":["Hack off all blown plaster up to at least 300 mm above the highest tide-line.","Inject a chemical damp-proof course at mortar joint level along the affected wall.","Re-plaster using a salt-resistant render system.","Confirm external ground levels and gutters are not contributing to the moisture path."],"homeowner_prep":"Move any furniture or stored items away from the affected wall so the contractor has clear access.","image_observations":[{"primary_observation":"Horizontal damp tide-line approximately 30 cm above the skirting, with peeling paint and salt staining.","components_visible":["plastered wall","skirting board","paint surface"],"components_missing_or_damaged":["paint (blown and peeling)","plaster (likely failed behind paint)"],"role_in_diagnosis":"primary_evidence"}],"clarification_questions":[],"diy_verification":"Press a square of cling film against the dry area of the wall, seal the edges with tape, and check after 24 hours; condensation on the inside of the film confirms moisture is migrating through the wall.","photo_request":"","confidence_drivers":["Classic horizontal tide-line pattern","Salt staining visible","No alternative source nearby"]}
